---
title: Trying a new workflow to determine cell markers
date: 2024-06-24
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

# Goal of this analysis

1. Attempting a different workflow to determine cell type markers
2. Using the new cell type markers for SingleR

# Files and directories

## Input

CCA RL file is found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240618/rl_cca.qs`

Tumour file is found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/icheong/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230510/mb_mnn.rds`

## Output

The output can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624`

# Process and Results

## Determining Markers

Using the `clustree` conda environment

Lets load in the required libraries
```{r eval=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(Seurat)
library(qs)
library(presto)
```

Currently, the cluster markers are not doing great. The delta values (i.e., the gap between the assignment score for the assigned label and those of the remaining labels) are very low when using SingleR to predict cell type (around 0.1 to 0.3). Furthermore, the heatmap for the Signature Matrix does not seem to be very distinctive. Therefore, we will try a different preprocessing approach before we find markers and apply those markers to SingleR as well.

Before we do anything, lets find the common genes that are in both the Aldinger and Sepp human datasets so that when we find the cluster markers, the genes are present in both datasets.
```{r eval=FALSE}
out_directory <- "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624"
rl_cca <- qread("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240618/rl_cca.qs")

DefaultAssay(rl_cca) <- "RNA"

ald <- subset(rl_cca, subset = dataset_name == "Aldinger_full_cerebellum_human")
sepp <- subset(rl_cca, subset = dataset_name == "Sepp_full_cerebellum_human")

counts_ald <- GetAssayData(object = ald, slot = "counts")
nonzero_ald <- counts_ald > 0
keep_ald_genes <- Matrix::rowSums(nonzero_ald) >= 10
ald_genes <- rownames(ald)[keep_ald_genes]

counts_sepp <- GetAssayData(object = sepp, slot = "counts")
nonzero_sepp <- counts_sepp > 0
keep_sepp_genes <- Matrix::rowSums(nonzero_sepp) >= 10
sepp_genes <- rownames(sepp)[keep_sepp_genes]

intersecting_genes <- intersect(ald_genes, sepp_genes)  
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
> print(length(ald_genes))
[1] 25752
> print(length(sepp_genes))
[1] 18744
> print(length(intersecting_genes))
[1] 14547
```

First we filter for the intersecting genes as well as human cells, then run NormalizeData(), FindVariableFeatures(n = 5000), and ScaleData(var.to.regress = "CC.Difference") on the RNA assay. Next we filter the object to only contain the 5000 genes. 

Lets see if this workflow gives cleaner results
```{r eval=FALSE}
rl_cca <- rl_cca %>%
  subset(subset = species == "human") %>%
  subset(features = intersecting_genes) %>%
  NormalizeData() %>%
  FindVariableFeatures(nfeatures = 5000) %>%
  ScaleData(vars.to.regress = "CC.Difference")

rl_cca <- subset(rl_cca, features = VariableFeatures(rl_cca))

rl_cca$new_clusters <- paste0(rl_cca$new_clusters, "_", rl_cca$broad_cell_type)
rl_cca$broad_cell_type <- factor(rl_cca$broad_cell_type)
```

### By Clusters
The first heatmap will be created separated by clusters. Let take a look at the number of cells per cluster first.
```{r eval=FALSE}
table(rl_cca$new_clusters)

                 Cluster_0_GCP                   Cluster_1_GC 
                          2315                           1021 
                 Cluster_10_RL Cluster_11_oligodendrocyte/OPC 
                          1579                           1286 
                 Cluster_12_RL         Cluster_13_endothelial 
                            69                            876 
          Cluster_14_microglia Cluster_15_oligodendrocyte/OPC 
                           667                            217 
                 Cluster_16_GC                 Cluster_17_GCP 
                           844                              8 
                  Cluster_3_GC                   Cluster_4_GC 
                          5049                            263 
                 Cluster_5_GCP                   Cluster_6_GC 
                            20                           2259 
                 Cluster_7_GCP                   Cluster_8_GC 
                           152                             46 
                  Cluster_9_GC                      UBC_0_UBC 
                          2676                           1668 
                     UBC_1_UBC                      UBC_2_UBC 
                          1780                           1434 
                     UBC_3_UBC                      UBC_4_UBC 
                           669                           1010 
                     UBC_5_UBC 
                           186
```

Lets remove clusters with less than 100 cells. Removing Clusters 5, 8, 12, 17
```{r eval=FALSE}
Idents(rl_cca) <- "new_clusters"
filtered_rl_cca <- subset(x = rl_cca, idents = c("Cluster_5_GCP", "Cluster_8_GC", "Cluster_12_RL", "Cluster_17_GCP"), invert = TRUE)

filtered_rl_cca$new_clusters <- factor(filtered_rl_cca$new_clusters, levels = unique(filtered_rl_cca$new_clusters))
levels_index <- str_order(
  levels(filtered_rl_cca$new_clusters),
  numeric = TRUE
)
filtered_rl_cca$new_clusters <- fct_relevel(filtered_rl_cca$new_clusters, levels(filtered_rl_cca$new_clusters)[levels_index])
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
cluster_markers <- wilcoxauc(
  filtered_rl_cca,
  group_by = "new_clusters",
  assay = "data",
  seurat_assay = "RNA"
)

write.csv(cluster_markers, file = paste0(out_directory, "/cluster_markers.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

cluster_markers %>%
    mutate(group = factor(group, levels = levels(filtered_rl_cca$new_clusters))) %>%
    group_by(group) %>%
    dplyr::filter(logFC > 0.5, padj < 0.05) %>%
    arrange(group, desc(logFC)) %>%
    slice_head(n = 20) %>%
    ungroup() -> top_features

write.csv(top_features, file = paste0(out_directory, "/cluster_top_markers.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

heatmap <- DoHeatmap(filtered_rl_cca, features = top_features$feature, group.by = "new_clusters", assay = "RNA", slot = "scale.data") 
ggsave(file.path(out_directory, "cluster_marker_heatmap.png"), width = 30, height = 30, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![We can see a clear diagonal of gene expression which is a good sign](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/cluster_marker_heatmap.png)

### By Annotated Cell Types

The second heatmap will be created separated by annotated cell types. Let take a look at the number of cells per cell type.
```{r eval=FALSE}
table(rl_cca$broad_cell_type)

        endothelial                  GC                 GCP           microglia 
                876               12158                2495                 667 
oligodendrocyte/OPC                  RL                 UBC 
               1503                1648                6747 
```
```{r eval=FALSE}
cell_type_markers <- wilcoxauc(
  rl_cca,
  group_by = "broad_cell_type",
  assay = "data",
  seurat_assay = "RNA"
)

write.csv(cell_type_markers, file = paste0(out_directory, "/cell_type_markers.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

cell_type_markers %>%
    mutate(group = factor(group, levels = levels(rl_cca$broad_cell_type))) %>%
    group_by(group) %>%
    dplyr::filter(logFC > 0.5, padj < 0.05) %>%
    arrange(group, desc(logFC)) %>%
    slice_head(n = 20) %>%
    ungroup() -> top_features

write.csv(top_features, file = paste0(out_directory, "/cell_type_top_markers.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

heatmap <- DoHeatmap(rl_cca, features = top_features$feature, group.by = "broad_cell_type", assay = "RNA", slot = "scale.data") 
ggsave(file.path(out_directory, "cell_type_marker_heatmap.png"), width = 30, height = 30, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![The diagonal isn't as clear here which is to be expected as within cell types there are cell states](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/cell_type_marker_heatmap.png)

## SingleR

Using the `scrnaseq_env` conda environment

Lets load in the required libraries
```{r eval=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(Seurat)
library(qs)
library(SingleR)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(SingleCellExperiment)
library(scran)
library(scuttle)
```

Loading in the Seurat objects.
```{r eval=FALSE}
set.seed(1)

# Loading in Seurat objects
rl_cca <- qread("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240618/rl_cca.qs")
tumour_srat <- readRDS("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/icheong/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230510/mb_mnn.rds")

out_directory <- "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624"
```

Lets follow the original workflow in order to get a comparison to our methods. This time I will remove clusters with less than 100 human cells as a preprocessing step. 
```{r eval=FALSE}
# Subset out mice cells and clusters with less than 100 cells, then convert to SCE object
rl_cca$new_clusters <- paste0(rl_cca$new_clusters, "_", rl_cca$broad_cell_type)

plt <- DimPlot(rl_cca, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE, group.by = "new_clusters")
ggsave(filename = "new_clusters_umap.png", plot = plt, path = out_directory, 
            width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

Idents(rl_cca) <- "new_clusters"

rl_sce <- rl_cca %>% 
    subset(subset = species == "human") %>% 
    subset(idents = c("Cluster_5_GCP", "Cluster_8_GC", "Cluster_12_RL", "Cluster_17_GCP"), invert = TRUE) %>% 
    # Add cell type to $new_clusters column
    as.SingleCellExperiment() %>% 
    scuttle::logNormCounts()


tumour_sce <- tumour_srat %>%
    as.SingleCellExperiment() %>%
    scuttle::logNormCounts()
```

### Default by Cluster

I will need to run this step on the HPC. Finding the pairwise difference is very slow to run. Running in parallel speeds it up.

Training the SingleR model and predicting tumour cell type
```{r eval=FALSE}
common <- intersect(rownames(rl_sce), rownames(tumour_sce))

trained_model <- trainSingleR(
  rl_sce[common,], 
  labels=rl_sce$new_clusters, 
  aggr.ref=TRUE,  
  de.method = "wilcox", 
  de.n = 20,
  BPPARAM=MulticoreParam(8)
)

qsave(trained_model, file.path(out_directory, "standard_trained_model.qs"))

predict <- classifySingleR(
  test = tumour_sce[common,],
  trained = trained_model,
  BPPARAM=MulticoreParam(8)
)

qsave(predict, file.path(out_directory, "standard_predictions.qs"))
```

Lets plot the heatmap
```{r eval=FALSE}
predict <- qread(file.path(out_directory, "standard_predictions.qs"))
columns_to_extract <- c("orig.ident", "subtype")
tumour_df <- as.data.frame(tumour_srat@meta.data[, columns_to_extract])

predict_heatmap <- plotScoreHeatmap(predict, annotation_col = tumour_df)
ggsave(file.path(out_directory, "standard_prediction_heatmap.png"), plot = predict_heatmap, width = 20, height = 20, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![The prediction heatmap of all the scores](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/standard_prediction_heatmap.png)

Lets look at the raw counts for each label
```{r eval=FALSE}
table(predict$labels)

                 Cluster_0_GCP                  Cluster_10_RL 
                           861                           2538 
Cluster_11_oligodendrocyte/OPC         Cluster_13_endothelial 
                            37                             28 
          Cluster_14_microglia Cluster_15_oligodendrocyte/OPC 
                           283                              7 
                 Cluster_16_GC                   Cluster_6_GC 
                            82                            623 
                 Cluster_7_GCP                      UBC_0_UBC 
                             9                           5186 
                     UBC_1_UBC                      UBC_4_UBC 
                         16875                           1205 
                     UBC_5_UBC 
                             1 
```

Lets plot the delta values
```{r eval=FALSE}
predict_delta <- plotDeltaDistribution(predict, ncol = 3)
ggsave(file.path(out_directory, "standard_prediction_delta.png"), plot = predict_delta, width = 20, height = 20, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![The prediction delta distribution. A low delta means assignment is uncertain.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/standard_prediction_delta.png)

Lets add the labels back to the tumour object
```{r eval=FALSE}
tumour_srat@meta.data$pred_standard_label <- predict$labels
```

Lets take a look at what the predicted labels looks like
```{r eval=FALSE}
plt <- DimPlot(tumour_srat, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE, group.by = "pred_standard_label")
ggsave(filename = "standard_tumour_pred_umap.png", plot = plt, path = out_directory, 
            width = 10, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![Its over predicting UBCs](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/standard_tumour_pred_umap.png)

Lets take a look at the cell breakdown
```{r eval=FALSE}
table(tumour_srat$subtype, tumour_srat$pred_standard_label)

      Cluster_0_GCP Cluster_10_RL Cluster_11_oligodendrocyte/OPC
  G3              0           955                             34
  G4              0           388                              2
  SHH           861          1195                              1
     
      Cluster_13_endothelial Cluster_14_microglia
  G3                      13                  171
  G4                       9                  100
  SHH                      6                   12
     
      Cluster_15_oligodendrocyte/OPC Cluster_16_GC Cluster_6_GC Cluster_7_GCP
  G3                               2            21           16             0
  G4                               5             2            4             0
  SHH                              0            59          603             9
     
      UBC_0_UBC UBC_1_UBC UBC_4_UBC UBC_5_UBC
  G3       1191      5605       637         0
  G4       3873      7307       214         1
  SHH       122      3963       354         0
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/cluster_barplot.R")

tumour_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "pred_standard_label", group.by = "orig.ident")
bar_plot_name <- "standard_tumour_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = tumour_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

tumour_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "pred_standard_label", group.by = "orig.ident", position = "fill")
bar_plot_name <- "standard_tumour_proportion_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = tumour_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

subtype_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "pred_standard_label", group.by = "subtype")
bar_plot_name <- "standard_subtype_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = subtype_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

subtype_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "pred_standard_label", group.by = "subtype", position = "fill")
bar_plot_name <- "standard_subtype_proportion_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = subtype_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![We know SHH tumours are more comprised of GCP cells. However, we see here that it seems to be predominately UBCs which suggests that the prediction model here is poor.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/standard_subtype_proportion_bar_plot.png)

### By Clusters

Lets take the top markers we got from the wilcoxauc and but them into a list of lists for each cluster/cell type. I also need to filter the markers to ensure the genes also exist within the tumour seurat object
```{r eval=FALSE}
cluster_markers <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/cluster_top_markers.csv")

# Filtering out marker genes not present in tumour srat object
tumour_genes <- rownames(tumour_srat)
filtered_cluster_markers <- cluster_markers %>%
  filter(feature %in% tumour_genes)

# Spliting into list based on cluster
split_list <- split(filtered_cluster_markers, filtered_cluster_markers$group)

# Keeping proper order
group_names <- names(split_list)
ordered_indices <- str_order(group_names, numeric = TRUE)

# Creating list of lists. Each list is a cluster and its corresponding marker genes
result_cluster_markers <- lapply(split_list[ordered_indices], function(x) x$feature)
```

Training the SingleR model
```{r eval=FALSE}
trained_model <- trainSingleR(
  rl_sce, 
  labels=rl_sce$new_clusters, 
  genes=result_cluster_markers,
  aggr.ref=TRUE
)

predict <- classifySingleR(
  test = tumour_sce,
  trained = trained_model
)
```

Lets plot the heatmap
```{r eval=FALSE}
# To order the data by the tumour subtype
columns_to_extract <- c("orig.ident", "subtype")
tumour_df <- as.data.frame(tumour_srat@meta.data[, columns_to_extract])

predict_heatmap <- plotScoreHeatmap(predict, annotation_col = tumour_df)
ggsave(file.path(out_directory, "cluster_prediction_heatmap.png"), plot = predict_heatmap, width = 20, height = 20, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![The prediction heatmap of all the scores](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/cluster_prediction_heatmap.png)

Lets look at the raw counts for each label
```{r eval=FALSE}
table(predict$labels)

                 Cluster_0_GCP                  Cluster_10_RL 
                           335                             34 
Cluster_11_oligodendrocyte/OPC         Cluster_13_endothelial 
                            21                             21 
          Cluster_14_microglia Cluster_15_oligodendrocyte/OPC 
                            67                              5 
                 Cluster_16_GC                   Cluster_4_GC 
                           380                             12 
                  Cluster_6_GC                      UBC_0_UBC 
                           143                            232 
                     UBC_1_UBC                      UBC_4_UBC 
                         26451                             34 
```

Lets plot the delta values
```{r eval=FALSE}
predict_delta <- plotDeltaDistribution(predict, ncol = 3)
ggsave(file.path(out_directory, "cluster_prediction_delta.png"), plot = predict_delta, width = 20, height = 20, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![The prediction delta distribution, Low deltas mean assignment is uncertain.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/cluster_prediction_delta.png)

Lets add the labels back to the tumour object
```{r eval=FALSE}
tumour_srat@meta.data$pred_cluster_label <- predict$labels
```

Lets take a look at what the predicted labels looks like
```{r eval=FALSE}
plt <- DimPlot(tumour_srat, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE, group.by = "pred_cluster_label")
ggsave(filename = "cluster_tumour_pred_umap.png", plot = plt, path = out_directory, 
            width = 10, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![Its over predicting UBCs](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/cluster_tumour_pred_umap.png)

Lets take a look at the cell breakdown
```{r eval=FALSE}
table(tumour_srat$subtype, tumour_srat$pred_cluster_label)

      Cluster_0_GCP Cluster_10_RL Cluster_11_oligodendrocyte/OPC
  G3              0             0                             19
  G4              0            12                              2
  SHH           335            22                              0
     
      Cluster_13_endothelial Cluster_14_microglia
  G3                      10                   11
  G4                       9                   55
  SHH                      2                    1
     
      Cluster_15_oligodendrocyte/OPC Cluster_16_GC Cluster_4_GC Cluster_6_GC
  G3                               5            50            0            0
  G4                               0            23            0            1
  SHH                              0           307           12          142
     
      UBC_0_UBC UBC_1_UBC UBC_4_UBC
  G3         10      8536         4
  G4        222     11551        30
  SHH         0      6364         0
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/cluster_barplot.R")

tumour_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "pred_cluster_label", group.by = "orig.ident")
bar_plot_name <- "cluster_tumour_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = tumour_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

tumour_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "pred_cluster_label", group.by = "orig.ident", position = "fill")
bar_plot_name <- "cluster_tumour_proportion_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = tumour_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

subtype_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "pred_cluster_label", group.by = "subtype")
bar_plot_name <- "cluster_subtype_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = subtype_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

subtype_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "pred_cluster_label", group.by = "subtype", position = "fill")
bar_plot_name <- "cluster_subtype_proportion_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = subtype_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![We know SHH tumours are more comprised of GCP cells. However, we see here that it seems to be predominately UBCs which suggests that the prediction model here is poor.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/cluster_subtype_proportion_bar_plot.png)

Overall, it appear that giving our own markers for each cluster doesn't not improve SingleR predictions, rather it makes it worse.

### By Annotated Cell Types

```{r eval=FALSE}
cell_markers <- read.csv("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/cell_type_top_markers.csv")

# Filtering out marker genes not present in tumour srat object
tumour_genes <- rownames(tumour_srat)
filtered_cell_markers <- cell_markers %>%
  filter(feature %in% tumour_genes)

# Spliting into list based on cluster
split_list <- split(filtered_cell_markers, filtered_cell_markers$group)

# Keeping proper order
group_names <- names(split_list)
ordered_indices <- str_order(group_names, numeric = TRUE)

# Creating list of lists. Each list is a cluster and its corresponding marker genes
result_cell_markers <- lapply(split_list[ordered_indices], function(x) x$feature)
```

Training the SingleR model
```{r eval=FALSE}
trained_model <- trainSingleR(
  rl_sce, 
  labels=rl_sce$broad_cell_type, 
  genes=result_cell_markers,
  aggr.ref=TRUE
)

predict <- classifySingleR(
  test = tumour_sce,
  trained = trained_model
)
```

Lets plot the heatmap
```{r eval=FALSE}
columns_to_extract <- c("orig.ident", "subtype")
tumour_df <- as.data.frame(tumour_srat@meta.data[, columns_to_extract])

predict_heatmap <- plotScoreHeatmap(predict, annotation_col = tumour_df)
ggsave(file.path(out_directory, "cell_type_prediction_heatmap.png"), plot = predict_heatmap, width = 20, height = 20, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![The prediction heatmap of all the scores](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/cell_type_prediction_heatmap.png)

Lets look at the raw counts for each label
```{r eval=FALSE}
table(predict$labels)

        endothelial                  GC                 GCP           microglia 
                679                 648                5884                1455 
oligodendrocyte/OPC                  RL                 UBC 
                271                1675               17123 
```

Lets plot the delta values
```{r eval=FALSE}
predict_delta <- plotDeltaDistribution(predict, ncol = 3)
ggsave(file.path(out_directory, "cell_type_prediction_delta.png"), plot = predict_delta, width = 20, height = 20, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![The prediction delta distribution, Low deltas means that the assignment confidence score was very close to the confidence score for the non-assignments.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/cell_type_prediction_delta.png)

Lets add the labels back to the tumour object
```{r eval=FALSE}
tumour_srat@meta.data$pred_cell_type_label <- predict$labels
```

Lets take a look at what the predicted labels looks like
```{r eval=FALSE}
plt <- DimPlot(tumour_srat, reduction = "umap", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE, group.by = "pred_cell_type_label")
ggsave(filename = "cell_type_tumour_pred_umap.png", plot = plt, path = out_directory, 
            width = 10, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![This looks a lot better](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/cell_type_tumour_pred_umap.png)

Lets take a look at the cell breakdown
```{r eval=FALSE}
table(tumour_srat$subtype, tumour_srat$pred_cell_type_label)

      endothelial    GC   GCP microglia oligodendrocyte/OPC    RL   UBC
  G3          175   424   443       649                 225   720  6009
  G4          199    54   170       300                  26   610 10546
  SHH         305   170  5271       506                  20   345   568
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/cluster_barplot.R")

tumour_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "pred_cell_type_label", group.by = "orig.ident")
bar_plot_name <- "cell_type_tumour_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = tumour_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

tumour_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "pred_cell_type_label", group.by = "orig.ident", position = "fill")
bar_plot_name <- "cell_type_tumour_proportion_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = tumour_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

subtype_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "pred_cell_type_label", group.by = "subtype")
bar_plot_name <- "cell_type_subtype_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = subtype_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

subtype_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "pred_cell_type_label", group.by = "subtype", position = "fill")
bar_plot_name <- "cell_type_subtype_proportion_bar_plot.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = subtype_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 20, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![This appears to be more accurate. We can see that the SSH tumour is mostly comprised of GCPs which makes sense.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240624/cell_type_subtype_proportion_bar_plot.png)

One thing that we can try would be using the `$broad_w_ubc_subtypes` column for predicting. This column has the broad cell types, but for the UBCs, it is split into the sub clusters. I can try with SingleR generated markers, or my own generated markers.
